releases.title: See how long — and find your way back
notes: Every timeline card now shows its duration at a glance — nights for a stay, travel time for a leg — so you can scan how long you’re spending where.
Opening an activity and tapping “back” now returns you to the step it belongs to, instead of jumping to the trip home.

releases.title: Remove an activity
notes: You can now delete an activity: tap the trash icon on it in a stay’s list, or use “Delete activity” on its detail page.
Deleted activities go to Trash, so you can restore them if you change your mind.

releases.title: A photo behind your stay
notes: Pin a photo to a stay and it becomes the background of that stay’s card on the timeline — so scrolling your trip shows the view behind each place you’re staying.
Open a stay’s detail, then tap the pin button on any of its photos to set it as the background; pinning another photo replaces it, and tapping again unpins.
Text stays easy to read over any photo — a warm scrim keeps the title, dates and status legible in both light and dark mode.
Travel legs stay clean and text-only; only stays get a background photo.
Claude can do it too: ask it to pin a photo (the new pin_image tool).

releases.title: Deeper budgets & detail pages
notes: Tap any travel leg or stay to open its own detail page — with its activities, photos, notes and everything editable, just like activities already had.
Budget “Where it goes” now flips between Estimated and Actual, so you can see planned spend versus what you’ve really paid.
New Budget Insights: cost per person (split two ways for now).
Mark a cost as “included in another ticket” (on a step or activity) — it’s hidden on the card and left out of the budget, so bundled fares don’t double-count.
Locations now use real Google Maps links: paste a Maps link when adding a step/activity, or let Claude set the exact place — with coordinates as a fallback.

releases.title: Plan it yourself
notes: Build your trip right in the app: tap “add step” anywhere on the timeline to insert a stay or a travel leg through a quick guided wizard — no need to ask Claude.
Add activities the same way: an “add activity” button on each stay opens a short wizard for the title, day, cost, booking status and more.
Setting a location is easy — just paste a Google Maps link (or “lat, lng”) and the wizard pulls out the coordinates.
Map links now open Google Maps instead of OpenStreetMap.
More things are tap-to-edit inline: cost estimates, activity dates, and each step’s dates and times — with native date/time pickers.
Status chips open on a single tap now (no more double-tap to change Idea → Booked).
Travel legs show the date and the time (departure and arrival), not just the time.
Photo & screenshot uploads are switched on — attach tickets and confirmations to any step or activity.
Added a sample packing list to the France demo trip so the Mine / Partner / Shared filters have something to show.

releases.title: Your trip, on a timeline
notes: A warm new look — light theme by default, a polished dark mode, and self-hosted typography.
Home lists your trips; open one to see it as a vertical timeline of travel legs and stays.
Each stay now shows its activities nested underneath, with 'book ahead' flags and map links.
Tap any activity to open its details and jot notes — a scratchpad per activity, with its own map link.
Tap a step's or activity's actual cost or booking status to edit it inline; costs show in the entry currency with a euro equivalent.
A Budget page totals everything in euros — estimated, actual, and projected vs your target — with a bar that turns red if you're heading over.
A Packing page: shared or personal items with a packed checkbox, quantities and categories, and Mine / Partner / Shared filters.
Attach photos and screenshots (tickets, confirmations) to any step or activity, with captions.
Deleted something by accident? Trash keeps removed steps, activities and packing items so you can restore them — or delete them for good.

releases.title: Infrastructure live
notes: Cloudflare Pages + D1 + Access are live; the site is gated by email one-time PIN.
GitHub CI is wired: PRs get a demo-mode preview, merges to main deploy production.
This note shipped through the PR pipeline itself — the parallel-session workflow works.

releases.title: Project skeleton
notes: Scaffolded the AI-first Cloudflare app: static SPA, /api Functions, shared domain core, D1 schema.
Included the remote MCP server (worker-mcp) so the app can be operated by talking to Claude.
Wired the GitHub parallel-session flow: branch -> PR -> gated demo preview -> deterministic merge.
